  "findings": [
    "Personnel standing directly in line with the tendon during active stressing operations (extreme blowout/recoil hazard)"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately halt all stressing operations",
    "Clear the danger zone behind and in line with the jacks and tendons",
    "Establish a secure exclusion zone with physical barriers and warning signs",
    "Conduct a safety stand-down to re-instruct the crew on safe positioning during post-tensioning"
  ],
  "rationale": "Standing in line with a tendon during active post-tensioning is an extremely dangerous, non-compliant practice. If a tendon, anchor, or jack fails under high tension, it can release catastrophic energy directly along the line of the tendon, causing fatal injuries. Operations must be stopped immediately to clear the hazard zone.",
  "references": [
    "PTI (Post-Tensioning Institute) Field Procedures Manual",
    "OSHA 1926.701(a)"
  ]
}
